meta_title: ScamGuard — alerts about phone scammers in Moldova
meta_description: Free Android app that monitors for signs of phone fraud and warns you. Works locally, no data transmission.
hero_subtitle: Free Android app for Moldova residents. Monitors for fraud signals and warns you when a scammer calls and asks for an SMS code.
how_subtitle: Three steps — and the app runs in background 24/7
step1_desc: Download the APK and install. Free, no registration, no ads.
step2_desc: One-time setup — the app will request necessary permissions and show you how to grant them.
step3_desc: ScamGuard runs in background. If you receive a code during a suspicious call — you'll see a warning.
features_subtitle: Simple and clear feature set without extras
f1_desc: The app detects when you receive a one-time confirmation code and correlates it with an active call.
f2_desc: Alert triggers only if the code arrived during or right after a call. Regular SMS codes don't bother you.
f3_desc: Recognizes SMS from MAIB, Victoriabank, MICB, Orange, Moldcell and others — shows who sent it.
f4_desc: Large red button in the alert ends the scammer's call immediately — no need to dig through menus.
f5_desc: All SMS processing happens locally on the device. Internet is only needed for update checks.
f6_desc: English, Russian and Romanian. Switched with one button in settings.
privacy_subtitle: We understand that an app working with SMS requires special trust
p1_desc: SMS analysis, history, contacts — everything stays only on your device. Nothing goes to the server.
p2_desc: Your SMS content never leaves the phone. The app technically has nothing to send — we have no endpoint to receive SMS.
p3_desc: The only network request — an anonymous hash on first launch confirming you accepted the terms of use.
screens_subtitle: Simple interface — even for those uncomfortable with tech
screen1_desc: You can see that the app is working. Safety tips are always visible.
screen2_desc: Loud visual warning at the moment of danger. One button — and the call is ended.
faq_q1: Why does the app need so many permissions?
faq_a1: Each permission is needed for a specific function. SMS reception — to analyze incoming codes. Phone state — to know when a call is active. Call management — so the «Hang up» button actually ends the call. Display over apps — so the warning appears over the call screen.
faq_q2: Will regular SMS codes (Gmail, bank login) trigger warnings?
faq_a2: No. The warning triggers only if the code arrived during an active call or within 5 minutes after the call. In normal situations (you get a code for email login, for example) the app stays silent.
faq_a3: Yes. In the «Settings» section there is a «Stop ScamGuard» button. The app is paused until you start it again. No data is deleted.
faq_q4: How much does the app cost?
faq_a4: Completely free. No ads. No in-app purchases. No subscription. A personal project to help family and neighbors.
docs_subtitle: Legal information and terms of use
docs_privacy_desc: What data is processed, where it's stored and what is sent to the server.
docs_eula_desc: Conditions for installing and using the app, limitations of liability.
footer_about: Free app for monitoring phone scammers. Works locally on your device.
